top_stories.title: The death of standing privilege in the age of AI agents
top_stories.summary: The article addresses how AI agents are emerging as highly privileged identities that traditional Privileged Access Management (PAM) controls are not designed to handle. The piece focuses on the inadequacy of standing privilege models in managing AI agent access, positioning it within the broader PAM and identity governance space.

top_stories.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
top_stories.summary: Microsoft released a massive patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs accounting for more than half of the total. The update includes two zero-day vulnerabilities among the elevation-of-privilege flaws, making it directly relevant to PAM and access control security.

top_stories.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
top_stories.summary: Specops outlines five methods by which a Zero Trust architecture strengthens identity security, focusing on limiting access and enforcing device trust. The article addresses stolen credentials as a leading breach vector and covers privilege escalation prevention and lateral movement blocking within an identity-first Zero Trust framework.

top_stories.title: Bringing governance and visibility to machine and AI identities
top_stories.summary: AppViewX CEO Archit Lohokare discusses the convergence of machine and AI agent identity management as a unified governance challenge in a Help Net Security interview. The conversation draws on his experience at IBM and CyberArk to address visibility and governance approaches for non-human identities.

top_stories.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
top_stories.summary: The FBI and Indonesian National Police dismantled the W3LL phishing network, arresting its alleged developer and disrupting infrastructure used to steal account credentials in over $20 million in fraud attempts. The W3LL toolkit was an off-the-shelf phishing kit specifically designed to bypass multi-factor authentication and compromise Microsoft 365 accounts at scale.

news.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
news.summary: The article addresses the growing threat of credential-based attacks and the detection model shifts cybersecurity teams need to adopt to counter them. The content focuses on identity-centric threats where attackers abuse legitimate credentials to blend in with normal activity, a core concern for IAM professionals.

news.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
news.summary: Fancy Bear (APT28) continues its global cyber espionage campaigns targeting organizations with varying levels of technical sophistication. Security experts recommend patching and zero trust implementation as baseline defenses against the threat group.

news.title: Anthropic tests user trust with ID and selfie checks for Claude
news.summary: Anthropic has introduced identity verification for its Claude AI chatbot, requiring users to submit government-issued ID and selfie checks, making it the first major AI chatbot platform to implement such measures. The verification process is positioned as a trust mechanism, though it contrasts with competitors like OpenAI's ChatGPT and Google's Gemini, which do not require similar identity checks.

news.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
news.summary: Google has made Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) generally available to Windows users in Chrome 146, a security feature designed to block session theft by binding browser sessions to the device. DBSC prevents attackers from hijacking authenticated sessions even if cookies are stolen, directly addressing a common authentication bypass technique.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on stolen authentication tokens to gain access to targeted systems.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" proxies real login pages in real time, relaying victims' credentials and MFA codes to legitimate sites while capturing them. The service bypasses traditional phishing defenses by loading actual brand websites rather than static copies, effectively defeating MFA protections through an adversary-in-the-middle approach.

news.title: TechJutsu launches browser extension to bring MFA to the web
news.summary: TechJutsu has launched a browser extension designed to bring multi-factor authentication (MFA) capabilities to web applications. No additional details about the product's specific features, target market, or pricing are available from the provided description.

news.title: Cisco just made moves to own the AI infrastructure stack
news.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix Security to bolster its identity capabilities as part of a broader AI infrastructure strategy. The acquisition is aimed at strengthening identity security for agentic AI environments, positioning Cisco's platform as a control plane for machine and non-human identities.

news.title: Show HN: Prmana – OIDC SSH Login for Linux with DPoP (Rust, Apache 2.0)
news.summary: Prmana is an open-source tool written in Rust (Apache 2.0) that replaces static SSH keys with short-lived OIDC tokens validated at the Linux host via PAM. The implementation uses DPoP (RFC 9449) to cryptographically bind tokens to the requesting client, differentiating it from other OIDC-for-SSH approaches.

news.title: Identity Protection in the AI Era
news.summary: The article outlines a proactive, identity-first security framework integrating visibility, threat detection and response, zero trust enforcement, AI protection, and threat intelligence into a unified model. The content addresses core IAM concepts including zero trust enforcement and identity-centric security approaches in the context of AI-era threats.

news.title: Google Chrome Update Disrupts Infostealer Cookie Theft
news.summary: Google has added Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) to Chrome 146, using hardware-based cryptographic keys to bind session cookies to specific devices on Windows. This directly counters infostealer malware that steals session cookies to bypass authentication, addressing a common method used to circumvent MFA and session-based access controls.

news.title: UNC6783 Hackers Use Fake Okta Pages in Corporate Breach Campaign
news.summary: A threat actor tracked as UNC6783 is conducting a corporate breach campaign using fake Okta login pages combined with social engineering to impersonate IT support staff and gain unauthorized access to corporate systems. The attackers use these credential-harvesting techniques to steal sensitive data and extort victim organizations.

news.title: To counter cookie theft, Chrome ships device-bound session credentials
news.summary: Google is shipping device-bound session credentials in Chrome to counter cookie theft attacks where infostealers extract and exfiltrate authentication cookies. The mechanism binds session credentials cryptographically to a specific device, making stolen cookies unusable on attacker-controlled systems.
